title: Apply Now: 2026 Internships at the U.S. Trade and Development Agency (USTDA)
summary: Are you looking to gain hands-on experience in global development, infrastructure, and international partnerships? The U.S. Trade and Development Agency (USTDA) is offering a range of internship opportunities for motivated students and young professionals eager to contribute to impactful projects in emerging markets. USTDA plays a key role as the U.S. government s first mover on critical infrastructure development , helping to advance shared strategic priorities while fostering sustainable economic growth worldwide. Through its internship program, you can work alongside experienced professionals and gain exposure to international development, trade, and policy implementation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Available Internship Openings 1. Office of Programs Internship Application Period: February 19, 2026 March 20, 2026 Department: Office of Programs Overview: Interns in this office will support USTDA s core project development activities, contributing to initiatives that drive critical infrastructure development in emerging economies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); 2. Intern, Office of Administration / Human Resources Application Period: February 19, 2026 March 20, 2026 Department: Office of Administration / HR Overview: This role offers experience in organizational operations, talent management, and HR systems that support the agency s global mission and internal workforce development. 3. Intern, Office of the Chief Information Officer Application Period: February 19, 2026 March 20, 2026 Department: Office of the Chief Information Officer (OCIO) Overview: Interns will assist with IT systems, digital infrastructure, and information management, supporting the technological backbone of USTDA s global operations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Why Apply? An internship with USTDA offers you the chance to: Gain real-world experience in international development and trade Work on projects that support infrastructure growth in emerging markets Develop professional skills in policy, administration, technology, and project management Build a global professional network within a U.S. government agency Contribute to meaningful initiatives that drive economic development and sustainability Application Deadline All internship applications close on March 20, 2026 . Early applications are strongly encouraged. To apply to any of the internship opportunities, Visit Here Take the Next Step If you are passionate about global development, infrastructure, and international collaboration, these internships at the U.S. Trade and Development Agency provide a valuable platform to launch your career. Prepare your application materials early, tailor your resume to the role you re applying for, and highlight your interest in international development and infrastructure. Start your journey toward a global career today. Discover more Internship opportunities Here. title: European Ombudsman Traineeships 2026: Paid EU Governance Opportunity for Graduates
summary: If you re an ambitious university graduate or a student in your final year seeking world-class professional experience in European governance, transparency, public administration, or EU affairs this is one of the most sought-after opportunities within EU institutions for career-minded individuals wanting to work at the heart of EU democratic accountability and public service. Hosting Institution European Ombudsman Overview: What Is the European Ombudsman Traineeship? Digital communication Case-handling Traineeship for persons with disabilities Candidates should be nationals of a Member State of the European Union No previous paid traineeship or work contract in a European institution Bachelors degree as minimum, solid training on EU law ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Deadline: 13 and 31 March Location: Brussels or Strasbourg Compensation: approx. 1400/m The European Ombudsman Traineeship Programme is a paid, full-time, one-year internship designed for recent university graduates who want hands-on experience inside a core European Union institution. The programme offers a unique opportunity to work directly on issues related to EU administration, transparency, accountability, citizens rights, and good governance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Trainees are placed in either Brussels or Strasbourg, depending on the needs of the Office, and work under the direct supervision of senior EU officials, usually a Head of Unit or equivalent. The traineeship combines professional responsibility, structured supervision, mentoring, and exposure to the daily functioning of EU institutions. This programme is particularly attractive for candidates interested in: EU law and policy Public administration Human rights and accountability European governance and institutional relations Transparency and citizens complaints mechanisms Aim of the Traineeship The traineeship is intended to: Provide practical, real-world experience within an EU institution Allow trainees to build on academic knowledge through professional tasks Develop skills in policy analysis, administration, communication, and institutional work Promote understanding of how EU institutions interact with citizens Each trainee works under: A line manager who defines objectives A mentor who supports daily integration and work A structured evaluation system (mid-term and final assessment) Duration of the Traineeship Standard duration: 12 months (1 year) Extension: Not possible Exceptional cases: Shorter traineeships may be offered in the interest of the service Start date is typically 1 September each year. Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria) 1. Nationality Applicants must be: Nationals of an EU Member State, OR Nationals of an EU accession or candidate country Eligible regions: Europe (EU + EU candidate/accession countries) 2. Educational Qualifications Applicants must: Hold a minimum 3-year university degree (Bachelor s level or equivalent) Degree must be in a discipline specified in the relevant traineeship call Fields commonly accepted include (depending on the call): Law Political Science European Studies International Relations Public Administration Social Sciences Communication and related disciplines 3. Language Requirements Applicants must have: Thorough knowledge of one EU official language, and Very good knowledge of another EU official language English is mandatory, as it is the main working language of the European Ombudsman. 4. Prior EU Experience Restrictions Applicants must NOT have: Completed more than 8 consecutive weeks (or 2 months) of paid traineeship in any EU institution ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Held any paid employment (temporary, contract, auxiliary, interim agent, SNE, MEP assistant, etc.) in an EU institution This rule ensures fair access for first-time EU trainees. Special Programme: Traineeships for Persons with Disabilities The European Ombudsman runs a dedicated positive-action traineeship programme for candidates with disabilities. Key Features Separate annual call (usually February) Candidates may apply simultaneously to general traineeships if eligible Open to: University graduates, or Students in at least their third year of higher education Additional Support Reasonable accommodation at the workplace Additional financial support of up to 50% of the monthly grant to cover disability-related costs Disability-related income does not reduce the traineeship grant Applicants must provide official proof of disability in an EU official language. Selection Procedure Application Timeline Call published: Normally February Start date: 1 September Final results communicated: By 30th June Required Application Documents All applications must be submitted via the online application system and include: Completed online application form CV in English Copies of diplomas/degrees (no certification or translation required) One-page motivation letter in English (required for some profiles) Incomplete applications are automatically rejected. Selection Stages Eligibility and profile screening Written test (if applicable) Interview (video call or phone) Selection is merit-based, with attention to: Qualifications Skills Motivation When candidates are equally qualified, the Office promotes: Geographical balance Gender equality Inclusion of persons with disabilities Financial Benefits and Allowances Monthly Grant Equivalent to 25% of the basic salary of an AD6 Step 1 EU official Adjusted by: Country weighting (Brussels or Strasbourg) Household allowance (if applicable) The grant may be: Reduced if the trainee receives other financial support Adjusted due to salary indexation Additional Financial Support Case-by-case lump-sum assistance may be granted Extra disability-related costs covered up to 50% of the monthly grant Travel Cost Reimbursement The Ombudsman reimburses travel costs at the beginning and end of the traineeship if not covered elsewhere. Covered Travel Modes Second-class train Bus or car-sharing Economy-class flight (for journeys over 500 km or sea crossings) Private car (kilometric allowance) Tickets are usually booked by the Office Up to 50 kg of luggage may be reimbursed (with approval) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Insurance Coverage Full sickness and accident insurance provided Optional insurance for: Spouse Children (at the trainee s expense) Leave and Working Conditions 2 working days of leave per month No cash compensation for unused leave Sick leave: Medical certificate required after 3 days Hybrid work rules apply Strict confidentiality and ethics obligations Supervision, Mentoring, and Evaluation Initial objective-setting meeting Mid-term performance review (6 months) Final evaluation meeting Dedicated mentor throughout the traineeship Exit interview with Human Resources Termination or Suspension Either party may terminate the traineeship One-month notice required for voluntary resignation Grant payments stop during suspension Due process applies in case of early termination Legal Status of Trainees Trainees are not EU officials or staff members No automatic employment rights after completion Data Protection All personal data is processed under Regulation (EU) 2018/1725, ensuring high standards of privacy and data protection. How to Apply Applications must be submitted online via the official European Ombudsman website once the call is published (normally in February). Important: Applicants may reapply in future years if unsuccessful. Apply here Key Deadlines (Typical Cycle) Call opens: February Application deadline: 13 and 31 March Interviews/tests: Spring Final decisions: By 30 June Traineeship starts: 1 September Why This Traineeship Is Highly Valuable Paid, long-term EU institutional experience Direct exposure to EU governance and citizens rights Strong CV value for EU careers, NGOs, public policy, and law Inclusive programme with strong disability support Work in Brussels or Strasbourg, the heart of Europe The European Ombudsman Traineeship is more than just an internship it s a gateway to public service, advancing democratic accountability and citizen rights within the EU. You ll gain practical EU-level work experience, expand your professional network, and earn credentials that can propel careers in governance, policy, law, human rights, and international relations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post European Ombudsman Traineeships 2026: Paid EU Governance Opportunity for Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNOY Internship 2025: Partnerships Officer Opportunity in the Netherlands
summary: The UNOY Internship 2025 is now open for applications for the Partnerships Officer position at UNOY Peacebuilders in The Hague, Netherlands. This UNOY Internship offers young professionals a valuable opportunity to gain hands-on experience in peacebuilding, fundraising, and partnership development within an international youth-led organization. Applications for the UNOY Internship close on 9 March 2025 at 23:59 CET, and selected candidates will participate in a six-month internship beginning on 28 April 2025. About the UNOY Internship Program The UNOY Internship at UNOY Peacebuilders provides young professionals with practical experience in the field of international peacebuilding and development. UNOY Peacebuilders is a global youth-led network consisting of more than 100 youth organizations across over 70 countries working together to promote peace, inclusion, and youth participation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Through the UNOY Internship, interns join a dynamic international team and contribute to real projects that support youth-led peacebuilding initiatives worldwide. Interns work alongside experienced professionals while building skills in fundraising, partnerships, research, communications, and project coordination. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); The UNOY Internship allows participants to expand their professional networks and gain experience within the international development and peacebuilding sectors. UNOY Internship Responsibilities During the UNOY Internship, the selected Partnerships Officer intern will work closely with the Partnership Coordinator and other team members to support various fundraising and partnership initiatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Fundraising and Partnership Development One key component of the UNOY Internship is supporting the organization s fundraising strategy and building relationships with partners and donors. Intern responsibilities include: Supporting the implementation of UNOY s fundraising strategy Researching potential donors and funding opportunities Identifying funding priorities and entry points for new partnerships Assisting in drafting concept notes and grant proposals Supporting relationship management with donors and partner organizations Contributing to reporting and grant management processes These activities provide interns with valuable experience in proposal writing and partnership management within the nonprofit sector. Financial Support to UNOY Members Another important component of the UNOY Internship involves supporting the Financial Support to UNOY Members (FSUM) program. This initiative provides small grants to youth-led organizations within the UNOY network. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Intern responsibilities within this program include: Supporting the management and implementation of the FSUM program Assisting with grant monitoring and reporting processes Preparing fundraising alerts highlighting funding opportunities for UNOY members Supporting monitoring, evaluation, and learning activities Through this work, interns gain insight into grant management and youth funding programs. Communications and Administrative Support The UNOY Internship also includes communications and administrative responsibilities that support the organization s partnership and program activities. Intern tasks may include: Producing and editing content for UNOY s website and social media channels Supporting communications related to partnership initiatives Assisting with planning major meetings such as the Annual Regional Coordinators and International Steering Group meeting Providing general office support where necessary These activities help interns develop strong communication and project coordination skills. Benefits of the UNOY Internship The UNOY Internship provides a range of benefits that support both professional development and practical experience in international development. Benefits include: A monthly stipend of 500 EUR Travel reimbursement for commuting to the office Hybrid work with the possibility of working remotely two days per week Opportunities to gain experience in international development and peacebuilding Access to mentorship and supervision from experienced professionals Networking opportunities with organizations and partners across the world The UNOY Internship also serves as a stepping stone for future careers in international organizations, government institutions, academia, and nonprofit development sectors. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Eligibility for the UNOY Internship To apply for the UNOY Internship, candidates must meet several requirements related to education, location, and experience. Applicants should have: A degree in peace and conflict studies, international relations, or a related field Strong interest in peacebuilding and youth leadership Excellent English speaking and writing skills Experience with data management tools and spreadsheets Strong project management and organizational skills Ability to work independently and within multicultural teams Knowledge of additional languages such as French, Arabic, Spanish, or Dutch is considered an advantage. Applicants must also meet legal requirements for working in the Netherlands, including being a resident of the Netherlands or a citizen of the European Union. Location and Working Conditions The UNOY Internship is based in The Hague, Netherlands, a global hub for international organizations and peace institutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); The internship includes: A six-month duration A four-day work week (Monday to Thursday) totaling 32 hours per week Hybrid working arrangements with two remote workdays per week Interns must also have access to a personal computer and secure internet connection. How to Apply for the UNOY Internship Candidates interested in the UNOY Internship must apply through the official online application form provided by UNOY. Applicants will be required to submit: A CV Answers to several short questions about their motivation and skills (maximum 250 words each) Applicants experiencing technical issues with the application form may contact the UNOY team directly via email. APPLY HERE Deadline The UNOY Internship application deadline is March 9, 2025 at 23:59 CET. Because applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, candidates are encouraged to apply as early as possible. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Final Note The UNOY Internship offers a valuable opportunity for young professionals interested in peacebuilding, international development, and youth leadership to gain real-world experience in a global organization. By participating in this internship, candidates can develop critical skills, contribute to meaningful peace initiatives, and build networks across the international development community. For more global fellowships, internships, scholarships, and leadership programs, click here .
